compiled_state.title: [RED] Write failing integration test for ticket quality-check dispatcher
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Write a failing RED integration test for the `ticket quality-check` subcommand before the implementation exists.

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-quality-check-dispatcher.sh (new)
- .test-index (modify — append to `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` and `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` entries)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-ticket-quality-check-dispatcher.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x)
- [ ] Test invokes `ticket quality-check <id>` through the dispatcher (not issue-quality-check.sh directly)
- [ ] Test uses a mock TICKET_CMD returning fixture ticket JSON with varying description lengths/keyword counts
- [ ] Test asserts exit 0 and "QUALITY: pass" output for a sufficiently detailed ticket fixture
- [ ] Test asserts exit 1 and "QUALITY: fail" output for a sparse ticket fixture (short description, no AC items, no file impact)
- [ ] Tests FAIL (RED) before implementation task T2 runs
- [ ] .test-index updated to add test file to both dispatcher and lib entries

## Red Marker
tests/scripts/test-ticket-quality-check-dispatcher.sh [test_quality_check_routes_through_dispatcher]

## Notes
issue-quality-check.sh uses TICKET_CMD to call `ticket show <id>`. Exit 0=sufficient quality, 1=too sparse. The pass threshold requires: description >= minimum line count, keywords present, AC items present, file impact section present. See issue-quality-check.sh for exact thresholds.
